summary.intent: Fix RFC 8252 compliance for loopback OAuth redirect URIs in the MCP OAuth provider, then extend the fix to shared infrastructure and provider-setup.
summary.outcome: Promoted normalize_loopback_redirect to a shared moltis-oauth crate, applied it in both MCP and provider-setup OAuth flows, committed the follow-up, and prepared a PR; a tracking issue was filed for the unresolved manual [mcp.servers.*.oauth] override complaint.

repo: normalize_loopback_redirect logic now lives in crates/oauth/src/redirect.rs and is re-exported from crates/oauth/src/lib.rs for use across MCP and provider-setup crates.
moltis_tls::serve_tls_with_http_redirect transparently bounces plain-HTTP OAuth callbacks to the HTTPS handler, preserving path and query — loopback normalization relies on this.
Non-loopback hosts (e.g. https://moltis.lan) are intentionally unaffected by the normalization helper.

build_auth_provider in manager.rs has a branch (~line 242) that skips discovery for manual [mcp.servers.*.oauth] overrides — this path appeared correct but could not be reproduced against a live provider.
provider-oauth.js uses ${window.location.origin}/auth/callback for redirect URIs; the provider-setup oauth_start use_server_callback branch is the Rust-side counterpart that was updated.

code.finding: New shared module containing normalize_loopback_redirect: rewrites https:// to http:// when host is localhost, 127.0.0.1, or ::1; hosts all 10 unit tests for the helper.
Removed local normalize_loopback_redirect implementation and its 10 unit tests; now imports from moltis_oauth::redirect. Retained the start_web_oauth_flow integration test (mockito-based).
Added import of moltis_oauth::redirect::normalize_loopback_redirect and applied it in the oauth_start function to normalize redirect URIs before passing them downstream.
Manual oauth override branch skips discovery entirely — logic appeared correct but needs a live repro against Attio to verify.

workflow: Tracking issues (bds) were filed for unresolvable complaints that lacked a reproducible test case rather than attempting blind fixes.
boulder-penalty branch is ephemeral with no upstream; push + PR creation was deferred to a subsequent step.
cargo fmt, clippy -D warnings, and cargo test were all run and confirmed clean before committing.

friction: The manual [mcp.servers.*.oauth] override complaint (from issue #629 sub-complaint) could not be reproduced without a live Attio instance, blocking a proper fix.
boulder-penalty had no upstream remote, requiring an explicit push step before PR creation.
open_items: Investigate and fix the reported behavior where [mcp.servers.*.oauth] manual overrides appear to be ignored — needs a dedicated repro against Attio (tracking bd filed).
Assess whether provider-oauth.js needs client-side loopback normalization in addition to the server-side fix applied in provider-setup.
Push boulder-penalty branch and create the PR (was committed but not yet pushed/PR'd at end of transcript).
